answer:
**No specific verse on prayascitta for forgetting the Gayatri mantra appears in the Āṅgirasa Smṛti.**

The corpus contains the full text of the *Āṅgirasa Smṛti* (slug: `unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1`), a single chapter of 1792 verses on dharma, rites, penances (*prāyaścitta*), śrāddha, adoption, impurity, and expiations.[^1] It discusses *prāyaścitta* extensively (e.g., for omissions in rites, neglect of death anniversaries, and other faults), often prescribing Gayatri recitation as part of remedies.[^2][^3] However, corpus-wide searches for phrases like "Gayatri"/"gāyatrī" with "forget"/"forgot"/"omission" + "prayascitta"/"prāyaścitta" yielded no matches in this work.[^4]

If this refers to a different recension or unattested verse outside the Falsafa corpus (37 works, including 10+ Indic Smṛtis), it is not covered here.

[^1]: *Āṅgirasa Smṛti*, ch. 1, full chapter.[https://falsafa.com/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/]

[^2]: *Āṅgirasa Smṛti* 1.88: "With Pañcagavya... he should offer one hundred and eight oblations with the *Vyāhṛti* and the Gāyatrī." (108 oblations including Gayatri as expiation for bride's menstruation during wedding rites.)[https://falsafa.com/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-0f489e]

[^3]: *Āṅgirasa Smṛti* 1.150: "For neglecting the death anniversary... a recitation of the Gāyatrī ten thousand times..." (part of expiation).[^https://falsafa.com/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-b061ae]; 1.155: "A... recitation of the Gāyatrī..." (general expiation).[^https://falsafa.com/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-2d11d2]

[^4]: Corpus search results (10 hits corpus-wide, none from Angirasa Smṛti).
